title: Nutshell Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Nutshell** is an SMB-focused sales CRM exposing a JSON-RPC 2.0 API at `https://app.nutshell.com/api/v1/json` (and a REST beta at `https://api.nutshell.com/v1/rest`). This connector тАФ `NutshellConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "nutshell"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) тАФ wraps the operational CRM surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | RPC methods | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Contacts | `findContacts`, `getContact`, `newContact`, `editContact`, `deleteContact` | List/get/create/edit/delete person records |
| Leads | `findLeads`, `newLead` | Pipeline opportunities |
| Accounts | `findAccounts` | Company records |
| Activities | `findActivities`, `newActivity` | Calls, meetings, emails, notes |
| Users | `findUsers`, `getUser` | Nutshell seat list (assignment, ownership) |

The connector normalises contacts + leads + accounts via thin dataclasses in `models.py`, surfaces standalone `async def` methods per RPC (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC тАФ all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::NutshellHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Nutshell API key

1. Sign in to https://app.nutshell.com.
2. Open **Setup тЖТ API Keys**.
3. Click **Generate API Key**. Copy the key value тАФ Nutshell shows it only once.
4. Note your **Nutshell login email** тАФ it pairs with the key as the HTTP Basic username.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP тЖТ Manage Connectors тЖТ Nutshell**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `email` (string) | Your Nutshell login email |
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://app.nutshell.com/api/v1/json` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `email` + `api_key` are present, then calls the JSON-RPC `getUser` method as a credential probe. On 2xx тЖТ `(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`. On 401 or RPC `error` тЖТ `(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which invokes `getUser` again as a lightweight authenticated probe. On 2xx тЖТ `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 тЖТ `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 5xx тЖТ `OFFLINE + AUTHENTICATED`.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Nutshell uses **HTTP Basic** authentication (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). The login email is the Basic username; the API key is the Basic password.

### Credential model

- `email` is the Nutshell login email of the user the key was issued for.
- `api_key` is the secret issued from **Setup тЖТ API Keys**. It is sent as the Basic password.
- No OAuth, no token refresh, no expiry тАФ rotation is done by re-running install with a new key.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `email` + `api_key`. Initialises `NutshellHTTPClient`. Calls `getUser` as a credential probe. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a placeholder `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")`. |
| `health_check()` | Calls `getUser` again тАФ cheapest authenticated probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called тАФ no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None тАФ credentials live in the persisted connector config. |

### Wire format

Every outbound request is a JSON-RPC POST:

```
POST https://app.nutshell.com/api/v1/json
Authorization: Basic base64(email:api_key)
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json

{
  "jsonrpc": "2.0",
  "id": <int>,
  "method": "<rpcName>",
  "params": {...}
}
```

Successful response: `{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": N, "result": <any>}`.
Error response (often HTTP **200**): `{"jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": N, "error": {"code": <int>, "message": "..."}}`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` тАФ never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `email` | string | yes | `self.username` | HTTP Basic username (Nutshell login email) |
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | HTTP Basic password (Nutshell API key) |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://app.nutshell.com/api/v1/json`) | Override for sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

`username` is also honoured as a legacy alias for `email` so already-stored connector sessions keep working after the rename.

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["email", "api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_NUTSHELL_BASE = "https://app.nutshell.com/api/v1/json"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `NutshellConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP + envelope parsing lives in `client/http_client.py::NutshellHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `email` + `api_key`; initialise `NutshellHTTPClient`; call `getUser` as a credential probe.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth тАФ returns a `TokenInfo(access_token=api_key, token_type="api_key")`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Calls `getUser`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Nutshell contacts, leads, accounts and reports counts. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`. Failures on any one resource type are recorded as PARTIAL.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(*, page=1, limit=50, query=None, order_by="lastName") -> List[Dict]`
RPC `findContacts` тАФ body `{page, limit, orderBy, query?}`. Returns a list of normalized contact dicts.

### `async get_contact(contact_id, contact_rev=None) -> Dict`
RPC `getContact` тАФ body `{contactId, rev?}`.

### `async create_contact(contact) -> Dict`
RPC `newContact` тАФ body `{contact: {...}}`. Returns the normalized created record.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, rev, fields) -> Dict`
RPC `editContact` тАФ body `{contactId, rev, contact: fields}`. `rev` required (Nutshell uses optimistic locking).

### `async delete_contact(contact_id, rev) -> Dict`
RPC `deleteContact` тАФ body `{contactId, rev}`. Returns `{deleted, contact_id, result}`.

children.title: Leads
children.content:
### `async list_leads(*, page=1, limit=50, query=None) -> List[Dict]`
RPC `findLeads` тАФ body `{page, limit, query?}`.

### `async create_lead(lead) -> Dict`
RPC `newLead` тАФ body `{lead: {...}}`.

children.title: Accounts
children.content:
### `async list_accounts(*, page=1, limit=50) -> List[Dict]`
RPC `findAccounts` тАФ body `{page, limit}`. Returns normalized account dicts including industry and territory.

children.title: Activities + Users
children.content:
### `async list_activities(*, page=1, limit=50) -> List[Dict]`
RPC `findActivities` тАФ body `{page, limit}`.

### `async log_activity(activity) -> Dict`
RPC `newActivity` тАФ body `{activity: {...}}`.

### `async list_users() -> List[Dict]`
RPC `findUsers` тАФ body `{}`. Returns all Nutshell seats.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Nutshell surfaces errors in **two** shapes тАФ the connector inspects both.

| HTTP / RPC | Mapped to | Behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| 401 Unauthorized | `NutshellAuthError` | `(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install/health-check; raised elsewhere. |
| 404 Not Found | `NutshellNotFound` | Raised; gateway returns 404. |
| 429 Rate limited | `NutshellRateLimitError` | Retried with exponential backoff + jitter; honors `Retry-After`. |
| 5xx Server error | `NutshellNetworkError` | Retried; surfaced after exhaustion. |
| RPC `error.code` in {-32000, -32001, 401} | `NutshellAuthError` | Auth failure embedded in HTTP 200. |
| RPC `error.message` contains "not found" | `NutshellNotFound` | |
| Any other RPC error | `NutshellError(rpc_code, message)` | Generic envelope failure. |
| Transport (httpx) error | `NutshellNetworkError` | Retried; surfaced after exhaustion. |

Retry policy (in `client/http_client.py`): `max_retries=3`, `delay = min(1 * 2**attempt + uniform(0, 0.25), 32)` seconds. The first attempt honors `Retry-After` when present.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
### `install()` returns `(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` with no message
The `email` or `api_key` install field was left blank. Re-open the install form and fill both.

### `install()` returns `Authentication failed: Invalid API key`
The key was rotated or revoked in Nutshell. Generate a new key from **Setup тЖТ API Keys** and re-run install.

### `health_check()` flips to `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`
Nutshell returned 401 тАФ the key is no longer valid. Same fix as install auth failure.

### Repeated 429s in the logs
Your tenant is hitting the per-key rate limit (60 RPM on the standard tier). Lower `rate_limit_per_min` to throttle outbound RPC calls, or stagger the sync schedule.

### `editContact` raises `Invalid params` or `revision mismatch`
Nutshell uses CouchDB-style optimistic locking: edits MUST include the latest `rev` value. Re-fetch the contact via `get_contact()` to get the current `rev`, then retry the edit.

### Tests fail with `ModuleNotFoundError: shared.base_connector`
Export `PYTHONPATH=/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/shielva-connectors/core:.` before invoking pytest, or rely on `tests/conftest.py` which inserts the path automatically.
